title: Sati-Loop: Alignment-Aware Contemplative Cybernetic Platform
author: Bose, Joy
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Sati-Loop v1.1 — Initial Architecture Release
This release marks the first stable version of the Sati-Loop design specification and supporting repository infrastructure.
Sati-Loop is an open-source hardware and software platform for alignment-aware contemplative neurotechnology. Unlike existing meditation devices that reward proxy physiological signals, Sati-Loop uses negative-only feedback, scaffold withdrawal, and transfer to unassisted practice as the only success criterion. It is designed from the ground up to avoid the contemplative alignment problem formalised in the companion paper.
What is included in this release 
 Design specification v1.1 (attached PDF) — complete hardware architecture, sensor stack, signal pipeline specification, safety constraints, transfer test protocol, non-optimisation constraints, and bill of materials for two prototyping paths
Signal pipeline scaffold: fast_features.py, slow_context.py, classifier.py, artifact_rejection.py
ESP32-S3 firmware scaffold including TDM gating for taVNS/EEG isolation
Transfer test protocol, calibration protocol, taVNS safety protocol
Safety folder with hardware interlocks and firmware safety rules
Issue templates including a dedicated safety concern template 
 Key architectural properties 
 Layer 1 classifier uses EEG-only features on 500ms windows — slow physiological metrics (HRV, respiration, IMU) are explicitly prohibited from Layer 1 cue triggering
The intervention engine never optimises directly against the classifier output
Scaffold withdrawal is mandatory — the system fades its own support as skill develops
Transfer to unassisted practice is the only success metric 
 Companion paper
Bose, J. (2026). The Contemplative Alignment Problem: Reward Misspecification in Closed-Loop Meditation Systems. PsyArXiv. https://osf.io/preprints/psyarxiv/
Project archive
Bose, J. (2026). Sati-Loop: Alignment-Aware Contemplative Cybernetic Platform, v1.1. OSF. https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/EGKU4
Status
This is a design and infrastructure release. Signal pipeline and firmware are scaffolded but not fully validated. No human studies have been conducted. See STATUS.md and docs/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md.
 SatiLoop_Design_Spec_v1_2 (1).pdf

Sati-Loop v1.1 — Initial Architecture Release
This release marks the first stable version of the Sati-Loop design specification and supporting repository infrastructure.
Sati-Loop is an open-source hardware and software platform for alignment-aware contemplative neurotechnology. Unlike existing meditation devices that reward proxy physiological signals, Sati-Loop uses negative-only feedback, scaffold withdrawal, and transfer to unassisted practice as the only success criterion. It is designed from the ground up to avoid the contemplative alignment problem formalised in the companion paper.

 Design specification v1.1 (attached PDF) — complete hardware architecture, sensor stack, signal pipeline specification, safety constraints, transfer test protocol, non-optimisation constraints, and bill of materials for two prototyping paths
Signal pipeline scaffold: fast_features.py, slow_context.py, classifier.py, artifact_rejection.py
ESP32-S3 firmware scaffold including TDM gating for taVNS/EEG isolation
Transfer test protocol, calibration protocol, taVNS safety protocol
Safety folder with hardware interlocks and firmware safety rules
Issue templates including a dedicated safety concern template 
 Key architectural properties 
 Layer 1 classifier uses EEG-only features on 500ms windows — slow physiological metrics (HRV, respiration, IMU) are explicitly prohibited from Layer 1 cue triggering
The intervention engine never optimises directly against the classifier output
Scaffold withdrawal is mandatory — the system fades its own support as skill develops

Bose, J. (2026). The Contemplative Alignment Problem: Reward Misspecification in Closed-Loop Meditation Systems. PsyArXiv. https://osf.io/preprints/psyarxiv/
Project archive
Bose, J. (2026). Sati-Loop: Alignment-Aware Contemplative Cybernetic Platform, v1.1. OSF. https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/EGKU4

This is a design and infrastructure release. Signal pipeline and firmware are scaffolded but not fully validated. No human studies have been conducted. See STATUS.md and docs/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md.
